title: Tragacantha picta
intro: Tragacantha picta, also known as Astragalus gummifer, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae, and is traditionally used in various folk medicines. The health benefits of Tragacantha picta include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Tragacantha picta is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to make capsules and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Tragacantha picta is a plant species that falls under the classification of subfamily Faboideae, within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. The genus Tragacantha is a synonym for the genus Astragalus, which is a group of plants commonly known as milk-vetches or goat's-thorn. The classification of Tragacantha picta is further specified as belonging to the family Fabaceae, with the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Astragalus. Within the genus Astragalus, Tragacantha picta is a specific species. The exact classification of Tragacantha picta may vary depending on the botanical authority.
uses: Tragacantha picta, also known as the desert broom or broom grass, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
